summary: Large grCastle decompilation PR replacing many placeholder functions and raw offset accesses with typed C for the Princess Peach's Castle stage logic. The work added detailed grCastle parameter/table structs, expanded Castle-specific Ground union variants, updated grcastle.h prototypes, and corrected lb_800103B8's return type to float. The diff shows matching-oriented code with typed offsets, static data tables, MWERKS_GEKKO-guarded rlwimi inline asm, dont_inline pragmas, and helper/wrapper functions where needed.

key_files.role: Main stage implementation; added ~1447 lines replacing placeholders with typed grCastle logic, local params/tables, dynamics setup, platform movement, spawning/camera/material/item-related behavior, and matching helpers.
Added Castle-specific Ground variable structs grCastle_GroundVars4 through grCastle_GroundVars12 and union entries, allowing grcastle.c to replace raw gp+offset accesses with named fields.

Updated prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures for functions such as grCastle_801CE054, grCastle_801CE3AC, grCastle_801CF868, grCastle_801CFBD4, grCastle_801D0298, grCastle_801D02B8, grCastle_801D09B8, grCastle_801D0BBC, and grCastle_801D0D84.
Changed lb_800103B8 implementation signature from void to float.
Changed lb_800103B8 declaration from void to float to match call/ABI needs.

what_changed: Implemented many previously placeholder-marked grCastle functions, including grCastle_801CD658, grCastle_801CDA0C, grCastle_801CDC44, grCastle_801CE054, grCastle_801CE19C, grCastle_801CE260, grCastle_801CE3AC, grCastle_801CE578, grCastle_801CE8E8, grCastle_801CE9E8, grCastle_801CEACC, grCastle_801CEF04, grCastle_801CF0F4, grCastle_801CF308, grCastle_801CFBD4, grCastle_801D0298, grCastle_801D02B8, grCastle_801D09B8, grCastle_801D0BBC, and grCastle_801D0D84.
Expanded grCastleParams from a mostly opaque block to a detailed offset-annotated parameter struct with fields through 0x140 and a nested grCastleParams_Entry entries[9] array.

Added local typed static data/table wrappers for constants such as grCs_803B7EB8 quaternion, grCs_803B7E9C Vec3, grCs_803B7EA8 dynamic entries, blink/y-offset/weight/target tables, and grCs_804D45E0 degree-to-radian constant.

Replaced raw Ground byte/halfword offset accesses in visible excerpts, such as gp+0xDE and gp+0xD4 through gp+0xDC, with typed fields under gp->gv.castle9 or related Castle-specific Ground variable structs.
Added Castle GroundVars variants in types.h for stage subobjects/state, including platform arrays, dynamics descriptors, timers, object pointers, and small state fields.
Updated stage code to pass Ground_GObj* into functions that previously had unknown or missing signatures, for example changing grCastle_801CF868() to grCastle_801CF868(gobj).
Corrected cleanup/iteration logic to operate on gp->gv.castle3.x1C[i].data and pass HSD_JObj pointers to grCastle_801D0D84 instead of using an earlier totalLength-like expression.
Added logic for dynamics archive public addresses named dynamicsdata_flag3, dynamicsdata_flag4, and dynamicsdata_flag6 and links them to JOBJs through grLib_801C9B20.
Changed lb_800103B8 from void to float in both source and header, despite the shown body still delegating to lb_800101C8; exact ABI motivation is implied by matching but not explicitly discussed.
smart_moves: Using offset-annotated structs made the Castle stage state much more searchable and safer than raw casts like ((u8*) gp)[0xDE] or *(s16*)((u8*)gp+0xD4).

Splitting the many Castle Ground state layouts into grCastle_GroundVars4 through grCastle_GroundVars12 preserved per-map/per-subobject interpretations without forcing one inaccurate monolithic struct.
Encoding small rodata arrays as named table structs, such as grCastle_DynEntries and grCastle_TargetTable, documents array sizes and helps stabilize data layout for matching.
The rlwimi bit manipulation was isolated under #ifdef MWERKS_GEKKO with a non-MWERKS NOT_IMPLEMENTED fallback, keeping the matching compiler path intact while acknowledging portability limitations.

The PR used matching controls such as #pragma dont_inline and wrapper/helper functions like grCastle_801CE3AC_dontinline and fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslate where the diff suggests inlining or call shape mattered.
Function prototypes were tightened in grcastle.h as matching evidence improved, reducing dependence on UNK_RET and UNK_PARAMS.
decomp_lessons: For stage files with many map-specific states, introduce multiple union variants in Ground rather than overloading one partially known struct; this supports typed accesses while knowledge is still incomplete.
When a parameter blob becomes important to many functions, progressively type every observed offset and keep padding explicit; this PR expanded grCastleParams with exact offsets and pads.
Small stage data tables should be given local wrapper typedefs when array lengths are known from use sites; this improves rodata matching and makes future references searchable.
Bitfield/bit-insertion instructions may require compiler-specific inline asm for exact matching; guard those blocks and document through surrounding typed assignments.
Header prototype cleanup is part of matching work: once a function is implemented, update callers and declarations to concrete return and parameter types.

Do not assume the most semantically nice helper call will match; the presence of dont_inline pragmas, fake inline helpers, stack padding, and wrapper calls shows this PR prioritized generated code shape.
naming_conventions: Stage functions retain address-based names in the grCastle_801Cxxxx/grCastle_801Dxxxx style until semantic names are known.
Static data symbols use the existing grCs_803Bxxxx and grCs_804Dxxxx address-based naming convention.
Unknown struct fields use offset-style names such as xD4, xD6, xDE, x110, x12C, and pad_x1C until semantics are confirmed.

New local structs use grCastle_* names with descriptive suffixes where possible, including grCastleParams_Entry, grCastle_DynEntry, grCastle_DynEntries, grCastle_YOffsets, grCastle_WeightTable, grCastle_TargetEntry, and grCastle_TargetTable.
Castle Ground union variants are numbered grCastle_GroundVars4 through grCastle_GroundVars12, matching the existing numbered pattern rather than inventing semantics prematurely.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used MWERKS_GEKKO-gated inline asm for rlwimi bit insertion on gp->gv.castle9.xDE.
Added #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop around at least grCastle_801CDF54.
Added grCastle_801CE3AC_dontinline as a wrapper around grCastle_801CE3AC.
Used PAD_STACK markers and unusual local temporaries/control flow in several functions, indicating stack/codegen matching pressure.
Added fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslate inline with HSD_ASSERT line/file manipulation and direct JObj translate assignment, likely to match inlined baselib behavior in one call site.
Kept some low-level casts between Ground state storage and other pointer/integer types where the exact union interpretation or ABI shape required it.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.text: Noted there was inline asm for an rwlimi/rlwimi call in one function and that it could not be commented on directly.
review_feedback.outcome: Acknowledged in discussion; no blocking review comments recorded in the slice.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.text: Responded that the inline asm was surrounded by #ifdef MWERKS_GEKKO, so they were not worried about it at the moment.
review_feedback.outcome: PR merged with the guarded inline asm present in the diff excerpt.

follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename grCastle_GroundVars4-12 fields from x-offset names to semantic Castle names.
Search for follow-up changes to remove or replace the MWERKS_GEKKO rlwimi inline asm in grcastle.c.
Search later history for lb_800103B8 return type corrections or callers that explain why it became float.
Search for subsequent grCastle PRs that finish semantic naming for grCastleParams fields and the entries[9] table.
Check whether fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslate or grCastle_801CE3AC_dontinline were later simplified after matching improvements.
